title: CUSAT CAT 2026 Exam Pattern (Detailed)

detail.title: CUSAT CAT 2026 Exam Pattern (Detailed)
detail.short_info: CUSAT Exam Pattern 2026 is released with 225 MCQs, revised +4 marking scheme for 900 marks. Check the detailed subject-wise distribution, syllabus, and marking for B.Tech admissions here.

content.text: CUSAT CAT Exam Pattern 2026 features 225 MCQs (Mathematics: 90, Physics: 75, Chemistry: 60) for a total of 900 marks in a 3-hour CBT, with +4 marks for each correct answer and -1 for incorrect (as per the revised scheme).

Cochin University of Science and Technology (CUSAT) conducts the Common Admission Test (CAT) for admissions to B.Tech and related programs. The 2026 pattern is released and available on the official admissions portal. It follows the Plus Two (Class 11-12) syllabus, helping aspirants prepare strategically for engineering and integrated science admissions.

rows: Cochin University of Science and Technology (CUSAT)

content.text: Test Code 101 (for B.Tech and select Integrated Programs)

items: +4 for every correct answer
-1 for every incorrect answer
0 for unattempted questions
Negative marking encourages accuracy
Minimum 10 marks required in each section for ranking

content.text: The syllabus is based on the Kerala Higher Secondary (Plus Two) curriculum and matches Class 11-12 CBSE/Kerala Board topics.
Algebra, Trigonometry, Geometry, Calculus, Vectors, Probability, Statistics, etc.
Mechanics, Optics, Electricity, Magnetism, Modern Physics, etc.
Physical, Organic, Inorganic Chemistry topics from Class 11-12.
Note: Download the full detailed syllabus PDF from the official site (admissions.cusat.ac.in). NCERT textbooks are highly recommended.
Preparing for CUSAT CAT 2026 requires smart planning because the exam has sectional time limits and negative marking. Here are some practical and effective tips:

items: Understand the Exam Pattern First -Know the number of questions, marks, and time allotted for each section (Maths 75 min, Physics 60 min, Chemistry 45 min). Practice solving questions within the given time.
Follow the Syllabus Strictly -Stick to Kerala Plus Two (Class 11 & 12) syllabus. NCERT books are the best base for Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.
Focus on High-Weightage Topics -Give more time to important chapters like Calculus, Coordinate Geometry, Mechanics, Electrostatics, Organic Chemistry, and Chemical Kinetics.
Solve Previous Year Papers -Practice CUSAT CAT previous years’ question papers to understand the difficulty level and question types.
Take Mock Tests Regularly -Attempt full-length mock tests in a real exam environment. Analyze your mistakes after every test.
Improve Speed and Accuracy -Since there is negative marking (-1), avoid guesswork. Focus on solving questions accurately instead of attempting all.
Make Short Notes -Prepare quick revision notes and formulas for Maths and Physics. Revise them daily.
Time Management Practice -Learn to manage time between sections. Mathematics needs the most time, so practice it thoroughly.
Revise Regularly -Do not leave revision for the last month. Weekly revision helps in remembering concepts better.
Stay Healthy -Take proper sleep, eat well, and take short breaks while studying to avoid burnout.
